what_it_does: Spawns a headless Claude Code round-trip (`claude -p`) for an approved build decision, parses the structured JSON result, computes the actual changed files from git content snapshots, composes a rich work instruction from the decision payload, and runs an unattended watcher that dispatches auto-dispatchable approved build-intents with a concurrency cap while loudly deferring or resurfacing crashed dispatches.
observations: Safe-by-default permission posture: default is `plan` (read-only); `acceptEdits` is opt-in only via `COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION` read at call time.
The real runner invokes `claude -p <instruction> --output-format json --add-dir <repo> --permission-mode <mode>` with a bounded subprocess timeout.
Ground-truth change detection uses `git diff --name-only HEAD`, `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`, and sha256 content snapshots, not the model's JSON self-report.

`build_instruction` enforces an approved scope line, a rich context block (address/symbols/context), governing module constitutions, and a standards block requiring design-system compliance, self-description updates, and green tests.
`LaunchError` is raised for non-JSON stdout, empty stdout, timeout, or missing `claude` binary.
Runners and diff runners are injectable for deterministic testing; defaults are the real subprocess and git commands.

`drive_dispatchable` is the unattended watcher: it reads resolve verdicts, dispatches approved AUTO-posture build-intents up to a cap, defers the rest loudly, and advances a coarse cursor only past consumed events.

`resurface_crashed` scans the event log for `decision.dispatch` claims lacking a terminal `decision.implemented`/`decision.verify` event and creates a new review item plus a durable `decision.crashed` marker.
`_governing_constitutions` walks scope-file parent directories to find the nearest ancestor `AGENTS.md`, excluding the root constitution.
`_compose_context_block` is a pure formatter that renders only present fields and emits a visible note for malformed context items without crashing.
standouts: Dual safety switch: `plan` default plus `wire_armed()` gate prevents autonomous self-modification unless deliberately armed.
Git content-snapshot delta correctly isolates a single run's changes even when the tree starts dirty.
Rich prompt composition from payload locus, neighbours, and bounded notebook context, plus a constitution-hop to module AGENTS.md files.
Unattended watcher relies on event-log durability for exactly-once and loudly surfaces both cap-deferred and crashed dispatches.
conventions: Env-gated config constants with both an import-time snapshot and a call-time live reader for permission mode.
Fail-loud exceptions, explicit wall-clock timeouts, and no fabricated success on malformed output.
Injectable runners for testability while defaulting to real subprocess behavior.
Pure formatter helpers with no network/embed/live resolution side effects.
Extensive inline design-rationale comments tying behavior to cross-file sections (W1-W7, X1-X15, H7-H8).
concerns: `drive_dispatchable` and `_is_dispatchable` import `posture`/`AUTO` from `runtime.governance`, coupling this file to another module's semantics not visible here.
`resurface_crashed` calls internal-looking APIs (`suite._emit`, `suite.inbox.surface_review`) whose contracts are not defined in this file.
`_default_runner` uses the module-level `CLAUDE_BIN` constant, which does not refresh if the env var changes after import, unlike `permission_mode()`.
The JSON wrapper fields (`is_error`, `result`, `subtype`) are assumed without schema validation beyond type checks.
`build_instruction` concatenates potentially large strings without an explicit length cap or truncation policy.
notes: This is the safe-by-default headless Claude Code launcher and unattended dispatch watcher for approved build intents, with git-ground-truth change detection and loud failure handling.
questions: What exact schema does `runtime.governance.posture` expect for consequence classes, and what are the AUTO/CONFIRM/SURFACE/LOCKED values?
What does `suite.dispatch_decision` return, and how does it convert a `LaunchError` into a loud re-queue without raising?
What is the precise shape of a context item (kind, address, pinned, text) as persisted by the minting stage?
What JSON fields does the `claude -p --output-format json` CLI actually emit in production?
How is `drive_dispatchable` scheduled or daemonized, and who supplies the `cursor` persistence?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring describes a thin, testable channel for governed, launched, verified implementation jobs, and the file implements instruction building, launch, ground-truth diffing, and an unattended watcher that respects safety gates and exactly-once event semantics.
apparent_themes: loud failure and no silent dead ends
intention_signals: The file is trying to close an autonomous build loop while keeping a human operator in the loop: only approved, scoped, auto-class build intents launch; actual file changes are verified against the repo; and any crash, cap breach, or malformed round-trip is surfaced loudly rather than dropped.
novelty: The combination of a plan-mode safe default plus acceptEdits opt-in, git content-snapshot delta for scope verification, and an event-log-based crashed-dispatch resurfacer in a single launcher module.
contribution: Provides the concrete, testable, fail-loud launch channel and the unattended watcher logic that turns approved decisions into executed builds, plus ground-truth change detection that a fused system would keep to verify scope and prevent model self-report drift.
summary_for_embedding: Headless Claude Code launcher and unattended dispatch watcher with safe-by-default permission gating, JSON result parsing, git content-snapshot change detection, rich instruction composition, concurrency cap, and crashed-dispatch resurfacing.
intention_for_embedding: To execute approved AI build intents autonomously while guaranteeing no silent failures, no autonomous self-modification by default, and ground-truth verification of what actually changed.

symbols.does: Returns the live COMPANY_WIRE_PERMISSION env value, defaulting to 'plan'.
symbols.description: Reads the permission posture at call time so a runtime env change is honored without a process restart; the safe default is 'plan' (read-only).

symbols.does: Returns True only when the live permission mode is 'acceptEdits'.
symbols.description: Production-trigger arming gate: the self-modify circuit is inert by default and only arms when the operator deliberately opts in via the env var.

symbols.does: Exception raised when a dispatch round-trip fails loudly.
symbols.description: Covers non-JSON output, timeouts, missing binary, and crashes; callers must surface or re-queue, never swallow the error.

symbols.does: Lists tracked and untracked uncommitted paths in a repo.
symbols.description: Runs `git diff --name-only HEAD` and `git ls-files --others --exclude-standard`, returning a sorted set of dirty paths.

symbols.does: Computes sha256 content hashes for a list of repo-relative paths.
symbols.description: Returns a dict mapping each path to its hash or None if absent; used to detect actual content changes rather than just path presence.

symbols.does: Compares a post-run content snapshot to a pre-run baseline and returns changed paths.
symbols.description: Ground-truth delta: a path is included if its sha256 moved, was created, deleted, or became dirty; correct even when the tree started dirty.

symbols.does: Captures the pre-build content snapshot of currently dirty paths.
symbols.description: Taken immediately before launch so `changed_delta` can isolate this run's modifications from pre-existing dirty state.

symbols.does: Formats payload address, symbols, and context items into legible prompt sections.
symbols.description: Pure string formatter; skips empty fields and renders malformed context items with a visible fail-loud note instead of crashing.

symbols.does: Derives the distinct module AGENTS.md paths that govern the declared scope.
symbols.description: Walks each scope file's parent directories up to the nearest ancestor containing AGENTS.md, excluding the root; returns sorted repo-relative paths.

symbols.does: Composes the full work instruction string sent to Claude Code.
symbols.description: Combines the approved spec, scope restriction, rich context block, governing module constitutions, and standards block; does not ask the model to self-review.

symbols.does: Spawns `claude -p` with JSON output and parses the result.
symbols.description: Real subprocess runner; enforces timeout, fails loud on missing binary or non-JSON stdout, and normalizes the CLI JSON wrapper to the launcher result contract.

symbols.does: Runs one implementation job from a recorded decision.
symbols.description: Builds the instruction, captures a pre-run baseline, invokes the runner, and attaches git-ground-truth `changed_files` plus the effective permission mode.

symbols.does: Determines whether a resolve verdict should auto-dispatch.
symbols.description: Returns True only for approved build-intent items whose consequence_class posture is AUTO; mirrors the gates used by `dispatch_decision`.

symbols.does: Re-surfaces dispatches that claimed but never reached a terminal event.
symbols.description: Scans the event log for `decision.dispatch` without a matching `decision.implemented`/`decision.verify` event, creates a responsive review item, and emits a durable `decision.crashed` marker.

symbols.does: One pass of the unattended watcher that drives approved auto-dispatchable builds.
symbols.description: Resurfaces crashed dispatches, reads resolve verdicts, dispatches up to the concurrency cap, defers the rest loudly, and advances the cursor only past consumed events.
